Powered by Maxxim

The agency in a box, behind your brand.

White-label Maxxim and deliver the full agency, strategy, brand, websites, content and campaigns, under your own name. You own the client. We run the engine, gate by gate.

What Maxxim owns and what you own

Maxxim owns

The engine

Structured intelligence, repeatable frameworks, and the operating-system logic that runs the method at scale.

The method

3D + Deepen, enforced by the product — the gate is built in, not bolted on.

The library

Approved output libraries, evals and quality control, so the machine meets the human's standard.

The community

A partner community whose judgement beats a lone operator's.

You own

The relationship

You win the work and own the client. Maxxim is the engine behind you, never the name on the door.

The judgement

You hold trust, taste and judgement — the 20% clients pay for. You sign off every gate.

The outcome

Your client owns what gets built, outright, on their own domain.

The engine enables. The human delivers. The engine creates leverage; the human creates trust. Maxxim never overshadows the operator in front of the client.

How it works

Plug the engine in behind you.

01

Bring the relationship

You hold the client and run the always-human Discover.

02

Run the method

The engine drafts Design and Deploy at AI speed; you sign off each gate.

03

Ship it owned

The client gets finished work they own. You get repeatable capacity.

The product

Three surfaces, one engine.

The workspace where the team directs Max, the dashboard where humans approve, and the client portal where clients watch it land — with the studios and the engine underneath.

Design Studio

The Design Studio is where a client's visuals get made — logos, brand imagery, collateral, video. The designer is Sal, directed in plain English and grounded in everything the client already has: their strategy, their brand, their briefs.

Social Studio

The Social Studio is where a client's social media gets made and published, run by Pip.

The full product tour
  • The workspace. The team works by talking to Max — Maxxim's AI partner — inside a Cowork plugin. Max carries the method, drafts every document, and drives the tools. Everything it produces saves to one secure store, so the work is never trapped on one machine.
  • The dashboard. Where humans track each client across the process, review and approve deliverables, and run the two creative studios — Design and Social.
  • The client portal. A clean, read-only view where a client follows their own project: progress, documents, and their Brain.

An engine runs quietly behind all three, handling the heavy lifting — image generation, website builds, publishing. Nobody touches it directly. The rule of thumb: Max does the words; the studios do the pixels and posts; the engine ships it all.

The Design Studio

The flow spells Sal's name. Start — choose what to make and describe it briefly; Sal expands the description using the client's brand and generates candidates to compare. Adapt — refine by plain instruction: bolder, add the wordmark, make a mono version. Versions stack, and any earlier one can be branched from. Lock — turn the approved mark into production-ready vector files, ready for anything from a favicon to print.

The Studio honours the gates: each asset type stays locked until its brief exists, and a human picks and approves every result. Sal generates; a person chooses.

The Social Studio

The process is Pip's name: Prepare — Pip drafts on-brand posts from the client's social strategy, with a tailored caption per platform and an optional on-brand image. Inspect — a human reviews every draft, tweaks the wording or swaps the image. Post — only once approved does anything publish, sensibly spaced rather than fired all at once.

Nothing goes out without sign-off. Editing an approved post returns it to draft, so a change can never sneak through unreviewed. Pip can also draft on a schedule — preparing the week ahead automatically — but the drafts always wait in the queue for a person. The AI keeps the channels fed; the human keeps them safe.

The client portal

Clients get a sign-in of their own — a clean, read-only view of exactly one thing: their project.

  • Files — their workspace: the full 3D Process with a live progress bar, and every document produced for them, ready to open or download as a polished, branded PDF.
  • Brain — their business's private brain: a personal assistant, Ari, that answers questions about their business, grounded in their own project documents and nothing else.
  • Help — a short guide written for clients.

The portal is deliberately calm. A deliverable reads as in progress until it is ready, then ready to view. Clients follow along in real time without wading through drafts or internal workings — and they only ever see their own work.

How client websites work

Maxxim builds, publishes and hosts each client's website, and every change is made by a person on the team — previewed privately, then promoted live. Clients follow along in their portal; they never need to touch hosting or code.

Every site lives in one of two states. Locked is the default: a modern, component-based site that the team evolves conversationally — change a shared element once and it updates everywhere. Unlocked freezes the finished site into plain, hand-editable pages for quick copy fixes and image swaps. Unlocking is a deliberate, one-way step taken when a design is settled.

The ownership story is simple. The domain is registered in the client's name. Branded email runs on it. The finished site is theirs — a real asset, live at their own address, not a rental inside someone else's platform.

The roster behind the names

  • Max — the AI partner. Runs the engagement and does the words: strategy, plans, copy, briefs, summaries. Max's name is a process: Map, Assemble, eXecute.
  • Sal — the designer, in the Design Studio. Makes the pixels. Sal's name is the process: Start, Adapt, Lock.
  • Pip — the social media manager, in the Social Studio. Pip's name is the process too: Prepare, Inspect, Post.
  • Ari — the Brain. Answers anything about a client, grounded in every document held for them.

Behind the names sits a roster of forty-three focused specialists. Makers each produce one deliverable — the audience analyser writes the audience teardown, the brand strategist writes the brand strategy. Advisors review rather than write: an expert panel spanning brand consistency, behavioural psychology, growth and conversion, accessibility, analytics, legal compliance and more, brought in to pressure-test the work before or after it is made.

Become a scaled partner, not a stuck one.
